desc: Provide safe, professional transport for Australian Embassy officials and staff in and around Tokyo. The role also covers vehicle maintenance, property support, and general administrative duties. This is an ongoing locally employed staff position based at the Australian Embassy.
responsibilities: Safe transport of Embassy officials and staff within Tokyo and surrounding areas
Regular vehicle maintenance and cleanliness
Administrative and property support duties as required
req: Pre-existing work rights in Japan required
Business-level Japanese required

desc: Lead social media strategy, content creation, and cultural event promotion for the Embassy of Switzerland in Japan. This full-time role supports the Embassy's public diplomacy and cultural engagement objectives. Fluent Japanese and strong English communication skills are required.
responsibilities: Develop and implement social media strategy across Embassy channels
Create and publish content in Japanese and English
Plan and promote cultural events and public diplomacy initiatives
Liaise with Swiss and Japanese cultural organisations
req: Pre-existing work rights in Japan required
Fluent Japanese and English required

desc: Support UK-Japan collaboration in science, technology, and innovation as part of the UK Science and Technology Network at the British Embassy Tokyo. The role covers research, stakeholder engagement, event management, and cross-team support. Flexible working includes one remote day per week and adjusted start/end times.
responsibilities: Research and Reporting (25%): Research Japan's S&T landscape and communicate findings to UK government stakeholders
Events and Visits Management (25%): Coordinate logistics for visits by UK officials, researchers, and industry representatives
Stakeholder Engagement (20%): Maintain regular contact with academia, industry, and government in both the UK and Japan
Team Operations and Administration (20%): Handle day-to-day operations, purchase orders, invoices, and budget monitoring
Organisational Priorities (10%): Work flexibly across Trade, Investment, Policy areas; provide crisis support during emergencies
req: Pre-existing work rights in Japan required
Fluency (C2) in English or Japanese, plus business level (high B2) in the other
International work experience
Strong organisational skills managing multiple tasks to deadlines
Research, analysis, and information summarisation ability
Proficient in Microsoft Office
benefits: Bonus equivalent to one month salary in June and December
Health insurance (kenko hoken), employee pension (kosei nenkin), employment insurance
Flexible working: remote day per week, adjusted hours, compressed hours option

desc: Oversee daily service operations at the U.S. Embassy Deputy Chief of Mission Residence in Azabu, Tokyo (10-min walk from Kamiyacho, Roppongi, or Roppongi 1-chome stations). The role involves supervising three staff, coordinating official events, upholding service standards, and bilingual (English/Japanese) support. Apply by email to ORERCT@gmail.com.
responsibilities: Supervise three Deputy Chief of Mission Residence (DCMR) staff
Traditional butler duties and official event service coordination
Staff training, development, and service standards maintenance
Cash account records management
Provide English–Japanese translation as needed
Anticipate and resolve operational issues proactively
req: Minimum 3 years hospitality service management experience in international settings
Proficiency in both English and Japanese
Strong team leadership and fair, respectful management style
Exceptional discretion and respect for confidentiality
Subject to 6-month trial period for first contract
benefits: Severance at end of each contract
Annual wage increase based on performance

desc: Prepare and serve American diner cuisine (breakfast and lunch) at Suji's Cafeteria inside the U.S. Embassy compound in Tokyo. Working hours are 6:00 a.m. to 2:00 p.m., Monday to Friday. Apply by email with CV and recent photo to sujiscafe@gmail.com.
responsibilities: Prepare and serve breakfast and lunch — American diner cuisine with a daily lunch special
Maintain food safety and kitchen hygiene standards
Work 6:00 a.m. – 2:00 p.m., Monday to Friday
req: Must already reside in Japan with a valid work visa
Working holiday and student visas not accepted
High school diploma required
Chef or service experience required
English-speaking required
benefits: Security background check covered by embassy
hours: Up to 40 hrs/wk (6 a.m.–2 p.m., Mon–Fri)

desc: Perform daily cleaning, laundry, and ironing duties and set up and serve at official events at the U.S. Ambassador's Residence in Minato-ku, Tokyo (10-min walk from Toranomon, Tameike-Sanno, Roppongi-1chome, or Kamiyacho stations). Apply to ORERCT@gmail.com with English resume and supporting documents.
responsibilities: Daily cleaning, collecting and sorting laundry, laundering, folding, and ironing
Arranging clothing, linens, towels, and napkins
Setting up and serving guests at official events — breakfast meetings, luncheons, dinners, buffets, and receptions
Maintain personal cleanliness, hygiene, and uniform at all times
req: Pre-existing work rights in Japan required
One-year contract, renewable
Subject to 6-month trial period for first contract
Must be physically fit to perform assigned duties
High attention to personal cleanliness and discretion

desc: Design and deliver Multilingual Learner (MLL) lessons for middle and high school students at The American School in Japan (ASIJ). The role also involves linguistic auditing, coaching staff on language support strategies, and leading co-curricular activities. Salary estimated at ¥7.7–8.8M annually on teacher scale; visa sponsorship available.
responsibilities: Design and deliver MLL (Multilingual Learner) lessons for grades 6–12
Conduct linguistic audits to assess student language needs
Coach and support classroom teachers on language strategies
Lead co-curricular activities related to language development
Collaborate with the curriculum team on MLL programme development
req: Minimum 5 years of relevant teaching experience
Teaching license required
Starts August 2026
Overseas applicants welcome — visa sponsorship provided
benefits: Competitive teacher-scale salary (est. ¥7.7–8.8M/yr for bachelor's or master's holders)

desc: Directly-hired ALT position with the Nagano Prefectural Board of Education. Nine positions available across Toshin, Chushin, Nanshin, and Hokushin areas for August 1, 2026 to July 31, 2027. You will be based at one senior high school 3–4 days/week and visit neighbouring schools 1–2 days/week. In-person interviews are held June 9, 2026. Apply by email to kyogaku-koko@pref.nagano.lg.jp with subject line '2026 DHALT'.
responsibilities: Assist with English instruction at prefectural senior high schools and special education schools
Base school attendance: 3–4 days per week
Regular visit school attendance: 1–2 days per week
Irregular special education school visits: a few times per month
Work hours: 8:30 a.m. – 4:15 p.m. (lunch break 12:15–1:00 p.m.)
req: Must already have a valid work visa — sponsorship not available
Native or native-level English proficiency required
Teaching qualification or equivalent experience preferred
Must be willing to relocate to Nagano Prefecture
Any existing employment contracts must end before August 1, 2026
Salary is fixed — housing allowance not provided
benefits: Health insurance, employee pension, employment insurance, workers' accident compensation
Saturdays, Sundays, and New Year's holiday off

employer: International School of the Sacred Heart (ISSH)
desc: Long-term part-time substitute covering maternity/childcare leave at the International School of the Sacred Heart (ISSH) — a private all-girls Catholic K–12 school in Tokyo representing ~50 nationalities. Starting August 11, 2026. Apply via the ISSH online application form; a Japanese CV may be submitted in addition to English materials.
responsibilities: Teach Japanese across several grades from 5–12
Assess students and report progress to parents and administration
Conduct parent/teacher conferences
Collaborate with the Japanese Department on curriculum development and documentation
Participate in special events and engage with international families in English
req: Fluent Japanese at teaching level required
Ability to communicate effectively in English with international families
Full background check required as a condition of any offer
Must sign ISSH Child Protection Statement on acceptance
Visa sponsorship unlikely for part-time substitute role — confirm with school

employer: International School of the Sacred Heart (ISSH)
desc: Part-time Social Media and Content Manager reporting to the Head of School at the International School of the Sacred Heart (ISSH) in Tokyo. Work closely with the Communications and Advancement Manager to share the school's story across digital platforms and at events. Apply via the ISSH online application form.
responsibilities: Manage ISSH social media accounts
Collaborate with faculty, Communications, Admissions, and students to source content
Capture photos and video at school events on and off campus
Prepare and post content on social media and other school channels
Contract and manage professional videographers for two annual onsite events
req: Strong business Japanese and excellent English communication skills required
Proven experience in social media and content creation
Irregular hours — schedule varies based on school events including evenings and weekends
Clean online profile consistent with Sacred Heart values
Full background check required as a condition of any offer
Must sign ISSH Child Protection Statement on acceptance

desc: Provide bilingual logistics and personnel support for the SEA3000 General Purpose Frigate Project team at the Australian Embassy Tokyo. Coordinate staff transfers including housing, schooling, and banking, and liaise with Japanese and Australian government stakeholders. Eligibility for an Australian Government security clearance is required.
responsibilities: Coordinate personnel transfers for SEA3000 project staff — housing, schools, banking
Bilingual (English/Japanese) administrative support to project team
Stakeholder liaison with Japanese government agencies and Australian defence contacts
Maintain project records and support day-to-day logistics operations
req: Pre-existing work rights in Japan required
Must be eligible for an Australian Government security clearance
N2-level Japanese or higher required
Experience in logistics, administration, or defence-related roles preferred
